books.title: Anna Karenina
books.author: Leo Tolstoy
books.summary: A socialite starts an affair with a cavalry officer, against a backdrop of wealthy family life in Imperialist Russia.

books.title: The Outlaw of Torn
books.author: Edgar Rice Burroughs
books.summary: A powerful knight terrorizes the aristocracy and protects the poor in 13th century England.

books.title: Memoirs of a Revolutionist
books.author: Peter Kropotkin
books.summary: A Russian revolutionary tells of his life from early childhood, his adventures as a geographer, and how he turned into an anarcho-communist.

books.title: Tombstone
books.author: Walter Noble Burns
books.summary: An embellished retelling of the town of Tombstone, Arizona, during its heyday in the late 19th century, featuring famous heroes and outlaws of the American Old West.

books.title: The Secret of the Old Clock
books.author: Carolyn Keene
books.summary: The sixteen-year-old daughter of a prominent attorney undertakes an investigation to search for a missing will.

books.title: Psmith in the City
books.author: P. G. Wodehouse
books.summary: A young man abandons his dream of playing cricket at Cambridge because of financial trouble and goes to work for a London bank, where he’s joined by an unflappable friend.

books.title: She Stoops to Conquer
books.author: Oliver Goldsmith
books.summary: Two young men visit the countryside: one to find a wife, and the other to avoid one.

books.title: Five Children and It
books.author: E. Nesbit
books.summary: Five children encounter an ancient magical creature who grants them wishes—all of which go comically wrong.

books.title: The Incredulity of Father Brown
books.author: G. K. Chesterton
books.summary: The third set of stories of a Catholic priest who is also an amateur detective.

books.title: Dracula
books.author: Bram Stoker
books.summary: An ancient undead monster terrorizes Victorian London.

books.title: Running a Thousand Miles for Freedom
books.author: William Craft
books.summary: The authors escape from slavery by posing as a slaveowner and his slave.

books.title: The Borough Treasurer
books.author: J. S. Fletcher
books.summary: When a retired detective is found murdered, two business partners in a small Yorkshire town become the prime suspects.

books.title: Letters Written During a Short Residence in Sweden, Norway, and Denmark
books.author: Mary Wollstonecraft
books.summary: Mary Wollstonecraft’s account of her travels through Scandinavia.

books.title: On Liberty
books.author: John Stuart Mill
books.summary: Mill’s famous essay that applies a utilitarian ethical system to systems of government.

books.title: The Divine Comedy
books.author: Dante Alighieri
books.summary: Dante journeys through Hell, Purgatory, and Heaven in order to receive salvation and to find divine love.

books.title: Jack Keefe Stories
books.author: Ring Lardner
books.summary: A boastful, nearly illiterate major league pitcher writes a series of letters home detailing the goings-on in his life and career.

books.title: Journey to the Center of the Earth
books.author: Jules Verne
books.summary: Prompted by a mysterious Icelandic manuscript, a professor and his nephew, with the aid of a local guide, adventure to the center of the earth through the crater of a dormant volcano.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: Thomas Hardy
books.summary: A collection of Thomas Hardy’s short stories, mostly ordered by date of publication.

books.title: A Confession
books.author: Leo Tolstoy
books.summary: A brief meditation on depression, philosophy, religion, and the meaning of life.

books.title: The Giant Raft
books.author: Jules Verne
books.summary: A former slave trader with an encrypted note schemes about its meaning, while a man with a secret past travels with his family down the Amazon in a giant raft to attend his daughter’s wedding.
